videos.title: How Sleep Shapes Decision Making
bullets: Sleep affects judgment by changing risk tolerance, emotional reading, and recovery capacity.
A consistent wake time is presented as the smallest useful experiment before optimizing everything else.
Worth watching if you want a practical lens on why tired evenings lead to worse choices.

videos.title: Building Better Weekly Reviews
bullets: The review is positioned as a trust tool: it makes hidden commitments visible before they become stress.
The useful move is choosing three outcomes for next week, not building a perfect task database.
Best for people juggling work, family, and side projects who need a repeatable reset.

videos.title: A Simple Prep System for Busy Families
bullets: The system favors reusable components over full meal prep, which keeps weekday choices flexible.
The strongest tactic is making the healthier option visible and easier than the default snack path.
Useful if you want practical family food logistics without turning Sunday into a production line.
